originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&__eckey_814` is never used.

sanitizedDescription: The value written to `&eckey` is never used.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  true)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 35ae2e259e4.c L10: eckey = malloc(sizeof(int)) is a dead store when the path SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto=T is taken and rand()%2=0, causing neither *eckey=30 (L12) nor *eckey=15 (L17) to execute. The assigned pointer value is never subsequently read. This is a real bug in normal compilation. The PC requires !_STDLIB_H (restrictive), __need_malloc_and_calloc=T (restrictive), and !__malloc_and_calloc_defined (restrictive) — all of which are stdlib.h internal header guards that are not set in normal compilation. The bug also occurs in standard compilation contexts, so the PC is severely over-constrained. New bug (no ERROR comment for this dead store).
